text: Management quality signals present a mixed picture under CEO Timothy D. Cook's leadership. The company employs 164,000 full-time employees and maintains R&D intensity of 9.43% of revenue in Q3 2025, indicating continued investment in product development [,]. However, external commentary raises concerns about strategic execution, particularly regarding AI positioning and competitive response []. Macro headwinds include potential tariff impacts, with President Trump preparing industry-specific tariffs alongside country-by-country duties that could affect Apple's global supply chain and pricing power []. Analyst commentary suggests iPhone demand was propped up by pricing and pull-ahead buying ahead of anticipated tariffs, indicating potential demand volatility []. The current ratio of 0.87 and debt-to-equity ratio of 1.54 suggest manageable but elevated leverage levels [,].
